title: Friday Sermon 25 October 2002
description:
Believe therefore in Allah and His Messenger and in the Light which We have sent down.  For Allah is Well-Aware of all that you do.

('The Holy Quran'.  Al Taghabun [Mutual Negligence]: 9).

Today’s sermon also comprises the remembrance of the attribute of Allah the Almighty Al Noor (The Light) and is the final instalment of this theme thereafter the subject of other attributes will be taken up, Inshallah.

There is a Hadith record in the Musnad of Ahmad ibn Hanbal that the Holy Prophet said: Whoever read the beginning and the final parts of Surah al-Kahf there would be light for him from head to feet and whoever read this Surah there would be light for them equal everything that is between the heavens and the Earth.

Hazrat Khalifatul Masih I states:

Just as one is in need of the moon and the sun so for spiritual guidance one is in need of the Divine revelation, the Prophet and the Divinely revealed books.

(Hazrat al-Hajj Hafiz Hakeem Maulana Nooruddin – Khalifatul Masih I.  'Haqaiq al-Furqan: iv'.  216).

The Philosophy of salvation is that those who establish a holy and perfect relationship with God become the manifestations of that light which has no decline, …

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  'Chashma e Ma'rifat'.  Anwar e Ahmadiyya Press, 1908.  'Ruhani Khazain: xxiii'. 418.  'The Essence of Islam: ii'.  267).

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20021025-UR.pdf
